hero.title: Carmel Eli
hero.subtitle: MSW · Clinical Social Worker
hero.quote:
A calm space for presence, responsibility,
and meaningful inner movement

about.label: About
about.heading:
Clinical Social Worker,
10+ years of experience
about.p1: I approach therapy as a living, ongoing practice. My work is centered on supporting people in developing awareness, emotional responsibility, and a deeper relationship with themselves and others.
about.p2: Through presence, reflection, and conscious choice, meaningful change becomes possible — not by force, but through steady inner movement.
about.p3: I believe that growth happens when we slow down enough to listen inward. In therapy, I accompany you in exploring patterns, building emotional clarity, and creating differentiation — allowing change to emerge naturally, at its own rhythm.
about.quote: "I bring the same therapeutic values into my personal life — practicing conscious choice, emotional presence, and balance within family and relationships."

timeline.desc: Occupational club supporting individuals with mental health challenges through daily structure and community belonging.
Coordinating care for deaf-blind individuals in residential and independent community settings.
Individual and couples work. Community theater project "Rising to the Stage" for people with and without disabilities.
Golan Heights, including co-leading a psychodrama group for children.

services.label: Services
services.heading: Personalized therapeutic paths
services.disclaimer: This practice does not offer discounts. The focus is on meaningful commitment and real therapeutic work.

items.title: Individual Therapy
items.desc: A space for self-reflection, emotional awareness, and personal growth. Focused on identifying patterns, strengthening inner stability, and supporting conscious life choices.

items.title: Couples Therapy
items.desc: Supporting couples in improving communication, emotional connection, and differentiation. Creating healthier, more stable relational dynamics through presence and responsibility.

items.title: Parental Guidance
items.desc: Practical and emotional support for conscious parenting. Strengthening connection with children while maintaining clear and healthy boundaries.

items.title: Collaborative Care
items.desc: Coordination with other professionals when the therapeutic process benefits from an integrated, multidisciplinary approach to support the client.

methods.label: Therapeutic Approach
methods.heading: The Flower of Life
methods.intro: Six interconnected dimensions of the therapeutic work — each circle flows into the others, forming a whole.

pillars.title: Processing Past & Present
pillars.desc: Working through what was and what is, with awareness and compassion.

pillars.title: Solution-Focused Therapy
pillars.desc: Change-oriented work grounded in real movement, not endless analysis.

pillars.title: Body–Mind Connection
pillars.desc: Listening to somatic wisdom as part of emotional awareness.

pillars.title: Systemic Approach
pillars.desc: Understanding the self within the context of relationships and systems.

pillars.title: Differentiation
pillars.desc: Staying connected while maintaining emotional autonomy and responsibility.

pillars.title: Self-Love as a Key
pillars.desc: A healthy relationship with oneself as the foundation for all growth.

credentials.label: Credentials
credentials.heading: Professional background
lines: Master's Degree in Social Work — Hebrew University (2019–2021)
Bachelor's Degree in Social Work — Tel-Hai College (2011–2014)
Advanced training in differentiation, mindfulness, and emotion-focused approaches

blog.label: Blog
blog.heading: Reflections and insights
blog.intro: A reflective space for exploring relationships, emotional awareness, and personal growth.

posts.title: The River Doesn't Force
posts.excerpt: Why resistance might be the most important information you're ignoring — and how to work with it instead of against it.

posts.title: Differentiation Is Not Distance
posts.excerpt: Understanding the difference between creating healthy boundaries and disconnecting from the people who matter.

posts.title: Your Body Knows First
posts.excerpt: Learning to listen to somatic wisdom and what your nervous system is trying to tell you about your life.

collaborations.label: Collaborations
collaborations.heading: Integrated care, whole person
collaborations.intro: Emotional wellbeing and physical health are deeply interconnected. Through selected partnerships with trusted practitioners, clients can receive integrated support — emotional therapy alongside physical treatment — when beneficial.

booking.label: Book a Session
booking.heading: Schedule your session
booking.intro: Sessions are offered online, worldwide. Times are shown in your local timezone.

contact.label: Contact
contact.heading: A gentle first step
contact.intro: If you feel resonance with this approach, you are welcome to reach out. The first contact is an opportunity to sense whether this space feels right for you.
contact.name: Full Name
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell me what brings you here...
